figures.caption: Fig. 2. Maj@k exact-match accuracy on GSM8K dev for k ∈ {1, 4, 16, 64}. AGPO shows larger gains at intermediate sampling budgets, indicating a better diversity– consistency trade-off.
Table 3. Cross-family results on GSM8K and MMLUSTEM (mean±std over 3 seeds) under equal token budgets that count probe tokens. Avg is the unweighted mean over the six backbone–task cells.
Table 5. Robust dispersion substitutes for σ̂. Accuracy (mean±std over 3 seeds) and stability metrics (lower is better).
Fig. 4. Batch accuracy versus adaptive temperature τt during training. ATS heats early uncertain batches and cools later, more confident batches.
Table 4. Leave-one-out ablations on key datasets. Numbers show delta w.r.t. AGPO (full); negative is worse for accuracy, positive is worse for stability.
Fig. 3. Mean KL divergence and adaptive clip radius εt over 300 training steps (3 seeds). AGPO keeps KL bounded while shrinking εt as training stabilizes.
Table 2. Throughput and wall-clock comparison on 2 × A800 80 GB GPUs for onpolicy methods only.

Fig. 1. Two-phase AGPO with ATS. A probe at τbase estimates group statistics (including reward dispersion σ̂); a train phase then uses τt and the adaptive clip εadaptive to update the policy via Eq. (7). This coupling yields exploration when uncertain and stable updates otherwise.
